title: Waste to Energy Advanced Solutions Raises â‚¬10 Million Led by Suma Capital to Accelerate Syngas Conversion to Methanol and SAF
content: Waste to Energy Advanced Solutions (WtEnergy), a Spanish company specialising in the energy recovery of waste and biomass through advanced gasification technologies, has closed a â‚¬10 million financing round led by SC Net Zero Ventures, Suma Capitalâ€™s Climate Tech fund focused on scaling growth-stage companies, with participation from Shell Ventures and existing shareholder Cemex Ventures. The investment will be allocated to the execution of WtEnergyâ€™s current project pipeline, the technological and operational standardisation of its gasification plants, the strengthening of the organization and the development of new Syngas applications, as well as the companyâ€™s expansion across Europe. European industry faces the dual challenge of reducing emissions in energy-intensive processes while efficiently managing growing volumes of industrial waste. Waste to Energy Advanced Solutions addresses this by developing and operating its own gasification technology, which transforms waste and biomass into Syngas. This circular process gas can replace fossil fuels in energy-intensive industrial processes and can also be processed to produce hydrogen, methanol and sustainable aviation fuel (SAF). WtEnergyâ€™s small and medium-sized modular plants (5â€“50 MW) are designed to be directly integrated into industrial facilities. This will help customers reduce their energy costs, lower COâ‚‚ emissions and advance their circular economy objectives by recovering waste that would otherwise end up in landfills or incineration.
author: Analysts Account
